addKey: OBD via Advanced Diagnostics Smart Pro, Autel IM508/IM608, or Lonsdor K518. Connect to OBD port (driver side under dash, left of steering column). Select Subaru > Immobilizer > Add Key. Ignition on with existing working key; follow tool prompts to register new 4D62 key. Up to 4 key slots total.
allKeysLost: OBD-based AKL via BIU (Body Integrated Unit) access using Advanced Diagnostics Smart Pro, Autel IM508/IM608, or equivalent dealer-grade tool. Connect to OBD port, select Subaru > Immobilizer > All Keys Lost. Tool communicates with BIU to erase and re-register keys. If OBD route fails on earlier years, BCM EEPROM read (93C86 type) may be required as fallback.
obdPort: Driver side under dash, left of steering column, above brake pedal — standard 16-pin OBD2
specialTool: Advanced Diagnostics Smart Pro ADS2330 | Autel IM508/IM608 | Lonsdor K518

addKey: OBD via Lonsdor K518USA (can pull PIN if needed), Autel IM608, or Advanced Diagnostics Smart Pro. Connect to OBD port, select Subaru > Immobilizer > Add Key. Ignition on, present existing working key near start area, then new G-chip key. Up to 4 key slots. No PIN required for add-key on most units; Lonsdor K518USA can read PIN if tool demands it.
allKeysLost: OBD-based AKL using Tango key programmer (Subaru 80-bit AKL via OBD emulator method), Autel IM608, or Lonsdor K518. Tango uses emulator to bypass need for working key and forces vehicle to learn new key via OBD. Alternative: BCM EEPROM dump (93C86 data), import to Lonsdor LKE generator, use generated emulator to power dash, then add key via OBD.
pinSource: OBD read via Lonsdor K518USA if tool requires it (optional)
obdPort: Driver side under dash, left of steering column, above brake pedal — standard 16-pin OBD2
specialTool: Lonsdor K518USA | Autel IM608 | Advanced Diagnostics Smart Pro | Tango (for AKL emulator method)

addKey: OBD via Autel IM608/IM508 or Advanced Diagnostics Smart Pro ADS2255. Connect to OBD port, select Subaru > Immobilizer > Add Key (Smart Key if applicable). Present working key near start button, follow prompts to register new G-chip or H-chip key. 2017+ models use H chip (8A) — same OBD flow but requires Autel IM608 Pro or Lonsdor K518 for H-chip support.
allKeysLost: OBD-based AKL via Autel IM608 Pro (with APB112 emulator for 2017+) or Lonsdor K518. For 2017-2019 H-chip models: reset PEPS (takes ~13 minutes), then add key by presenting new H-chip key near start button within 30 seconds. Remove horn relay to prevent continuous alarm during reset. If OBD method fails, BCM EEPROM (93C86) dump and LKE generation required.
obdPort: Driver side under dash, left of steering column, above brake pedal — standard 16-pin OBD2
specialTool: Autel IM608 Pro + APB112 emulator (2017+) | Advanced Diagnostics Smart Pro ADS2255 | Lonsdor K518

addKey: OBD via Autel IM608 Pro or Lonsdor K518. Connect to OBD port, select Subaru > Smart Key > Add Key. Remove horn relay before starting (prevents 15-minute alarm). Present existing key near start button (one beep), then new HYQ14AHK H-chip key near start button. Up to 4 key slots.
allKeysLost: OBD-based AKL via Autel IM608/IM508 or Lonsdor K518. Two-step: (1) Reset Smart Box (remove horn relay first; ~15-min process); (2) Add key by presenting new H-chip key near start button within 60 seconds when prompted. APB112 smart key emulator used with Autel to generate initial 'analog key' for ignition. Network connection required. ABRITES AVDI with SB002/SB003 licenses also supported; SB003 handles faulty ESL via bench-level RH850 programmer for worst-case scenarios.
obdPort: Driver side under dash, left of steering column, above brake pedal — standard 16-pin OBD2

Verify chip type before programming: 2004-2007 typically 4D62 40-bit; 2008-2011 may be 4D62 40-bit or G-chip 80-bit variant.

onboard: DIY key-dance with 2 working keys: (1) Insert key 1, turn ON for 1+ sec until red key light extinguishes, remove within 5 sec. (2) Insert key 2, turn ON for 1-5 sec, remove within 10 sec. (3) Insert new unprogrammed key, turn ON — red key light illuminates then extinguishes = success. Max 3 keys. Does NOT work if only 1 key available. Applies to MAZ24 transponder and flip key types.
addKey: Use onboard key-dance procedure (requires 2 existing working keys). For remote/RF portion of flip key only (not transponder chip): with 2 working keys, enter programming mode then press remote buttons twice — up to 3 remotes total. For transponder programming without 2 keys: requires professional OBD tool (Autel IM508, VVDI2, or Advanced Diagnostics Smart Pro). FORScan with OBD adapter can add transponder key if 1 working key exists (extended license required).
allKeysLost: Professional OBD tool required: Autel IM508/IM608 or OBDSTAR X300 MINI (Ford/Mazda AKL specialist). FORScan with extended license can program transponder if at least 1 key exists. For true AKL: use Autel IM508 > Immobilizer > Mazda > Manual Selection > model > Transponder > All Keys Lost. Tool erases existing keys, programs 2 new 4D63 80-bit keys via OBD. Note: 40-bit vs 80-bit chip mismatch can cause failures — verify chip before programming.
obdPort: Driver side under dash, above pedals — standard 16-pin OBD2, no panel removal needed
specialTool: Autel IM508/IM608 | OBDSTAR X300 MINI | VVDI2 | Advanced Diagnostics Smart Pro | FORScan (1-key add only)

addKey: OBD via Autel IM608, Lonsdor K518, or Advanced Diagnostics Smart Pro. Connect to OBD port (driver side under dash, above pedals). Select Mazda > Smart Key > Add Key (or guided wizard). Tool detects HITAG Pro ID49 system, prompts to present new key near Start button when indicator goes green. No PIN required.
allKeysLost: OBD-based AKL via Autel IM608 II or Lonsdor K518. Navigate: Immobilizer > Mazda > Manual Selection > Japan > Mazda 3 > Smart Key > Keyless System (CAN) > All Smart Keys Lost. System erases all keys (~7 min process), then programs minimum 2 new HITAG Pro ID49 (WAZSKE13D01) keys by proximity to Start button. Network connection may be required. No EEPROM required for standard AKL.
obdPort: Driver side under dash, above pedals — standard 16-pin OBD2, no panel removal needed
specialTool: Autel IM608 II | Lonsdor K518 | Advanced Diagnostics Smart Pro

addKey: OBD via Autel IM608 Pro or Lonsdor K518 (free PIN via OBD). Connect to OBD port (driver side under dash, above pedals). Select Mazda > Smart Key > Add Key. When prompted, hold Start button 10 seconds, then press once when indicator changes green to yellow. Present new HITAG AES (WAZSKE11D01) key near Start button when indicator turns green — wait for beep. Minimum 2 keys required to complete.
allKeysLost: OBD-based AKL via Lonsdor K518 (free PIN) or Autel IM608 Pro. Lonsdor path: Immo & Remote > Mazda > Axela > 2020- > All Smart Key Lost. Hold Start button 10 sec then press once; wait ~7 min security access; system deletes existing keys; program each new WAZSKE11D01 key by proximity to Start button (green indicator). Test engine start with each key. Minimum 2 keys required. No EEPROM needed for standard OBD AKL.
pinSource: OBD read (Lonsdor K518 provides security access automatically — no PIN purchase needed)
obdPort: Driver side under dash, above pedals — standard 16-pin OBD2, no panel removal needed
specialTool: Autel IM608 Pro | Lonsdor K518

When prompted, hold Start button 10 sec, press once when indicator changes color. Present new HITAG AES (WAZSKE11D01) key near Start button when green indicator appears. Minimum 2 keys required.
allKeysLost: OBD-based AKL via Lonsdor K518 (free PIN, no EEPROM) or Autel IM608 Pro. Shares key system with Mazda3 2019+ and CX-50. Navigate: Immo & Remote > Mazda > CX-30 > 2020- > All Smart Key Lost. Hold Start button 10 sec then press once; wait ~7 min security access; program minimum 2 new WAZSKE11D01 keys by proximity. No EEPROM required for standard AKL.
pinSource: OBD read (Lonsdor K518 provides security access automatically — no PIN purchase needed)

Present new HITAG AES (WAZSKE11D01) key near Start button when prompted. Minimum 2 keys required. Shares key system with CX-30 and Mazda3 2019+. No PIN needed for Autel/Lonsdor.
allKeysLost: OBD-based AKL via Lonsdor K518 (free PIN) or Autel IM608 Pro. Same procedure as CX-30 and Mazda3 2019+ (WAZSKE11D01 / HITAG AES 6A chip system). ~7 min security access during AKL. Program minimum 2 new keys. No EEPROM required for standard AKL.

onboard: DIY key-dance with 2 working keys: (1) Insert key 1, turn ON for 1+ sec until red key light extinguishes, remove within 5 sec. (2) Insert key 2, turn ON for 1-5 sec, remove within 10 sec. (3) Insert new unprogrammed key, turn ON — red key light illuminates then extinguishes = success. Max 3 keys. Requires 2 existing working keys (NC generation MX-5).
addKey: Use onboard key-dance procedure (2 existing working keys required). For transponder-only with fewer than 2 keys: professional OBD tool required (Autel IM508, VVDI2, or Advanced Diagnostics Smart Pro).
allKeysLost: Professional OBD tool required: Autel IM508/IM608 or VVDI2. Navigate to Mazda > Transponder > All Keys Lost. Programs 2 new 4D63 80-bit keys via OBD. No PIN required.

specialTool: Autel IM508/IM608 | VVDI2 | Advanced Diagnostics Smart Pro
